title: jacobaea echaeta
side_effects: Jacobaea echaeta can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems. If ingested, it may cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure can lead to liver and kidney damage. Prolonged skin contact with the plant's sap may cause blisters, rashes, and inflammation. In severe cases, improper use of Jacobaea echaeta can lead to anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction.
